channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: FG settles N333bn GenCos debt, pays first bond coupon
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has settled N333bn of GenCos debt and paid the first bond coupon, restoring investor confidence in Nigeria’s power sector. Learn mor
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-settles-n333bn-gencos-debt-pays-first-bond-coupon/

item.title: I’m depressed, says Chidimma Adetshina as S’Africa court delays deportation ruling
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Chidimma Adetshina, former Miss Universe Nigeria, expresses depression as a South African court delays judgment on her deportation case until August 19.
Read More: https://punchng.com/im-depressed-says-chidimma-adetshina-as-safrica-court-delays-deportation-ruling/

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Mourners gathered in Lagos to bid farewell to Toba Ajiboye, popularly known as Toba Ijaya, the slain NURTW official. See emotional scenes from the Toba Ija

item.title: Premier League referee Taylor retires
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Long-serving Premier League referee Anthony Taylor is standing down after 16 years in England's top flight at the conclusion of the World Cup.

item.description:

item.description:

item.title: Anambra Police Command Agog As CP Orutugu Decorated As AIG
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Anambra State Police Command is currently agog with celebration following the decoration of its Commissioner of Police, Ikioye Orutugu, as an Assistant Inspector General of Police (AIG) on Tuesday. Recall that AIG Orutugu was among the senior police officers who sat for the promotional examinati

item.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

item.title: Insecurity: LG Chairman Issues Stern Warning To Security Corps
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Chairman of Akoko-Edo Local Government Area, Alabi Oshionogue, has issued a stern warning to members of the Edo State Security Corps (ESSC), insisting that the persistent wave of insecurity in the area is unacceptable despite the resources committed to grassroots security. Oshionogue spoke durin

item.title: 2027: Ebonyi ADC chairman, others seek extension of INEC voter registration exercise
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: ‎ ‎Political parties and other stakeholders in Ebonyi State have called on the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, to extend the ongoing Continuous Voter Registration, CVR, exercise. They warned that thousands of eligible voters, particularly in rural communities, risk being disenfranch

item.title: Your Time’s Up, Resign – La Liga Boss Tells Infantino
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: La Liga president Javier Tebas has called on FIFA president Gianni Infantino to step down, accusing football’s world governing body of “destroying the football industry.” Speaking to Italian newspaper La Gazzetta dello Sport, Tebas said when asked directly whether Infantino should

item.title: Court orders EFCC to produce estate agent for trial
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Abuja Federal High Court orders EFCC to produce estate agent Rebecca Omokamo for trial over alleged land fraud and money laundering. Get the details here.

item.title: NSCDC Busts Alleged Fake University In Lagos, Rescues 106 Students
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) has uncovered an alleged fake university operating from a three-bedroom apartment in Ilado, Olorunda Local Council Development Area of Lagos State, rescuing 106 young people believed to have been lured into the scheme. The operation, carried out i

item.title: Lagos Market Fire Guts 17 Shops
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The Lagos State Fire and Rescue Service said the cause of the fire is yet to be determined and that an investigation has commenced to establish the circumstances surrounding the outbreak. The post Lagos Market Fire Guts 17 Shops appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: NPA announces arrival of 26 ships at Lagos ports
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigerian Ports Authority, NPA, has announced that 26 cargo vessels carrying petroleum products, food items and other commodities are expected to arrive at Apapa Port, Tin Can Island Port and Lekki Deep Sea Port in Lagos between July 21 and July 26. The disclosure was contained in

item.title: 'Pressure is intense and scrutiny constant' - referee Taylor retires
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Anthony Taylor announces his retirement from refereeing with immediate effect - ending a 20-year career.

item.title: No political opponent can match Tinubu, Hamzat’s credentials — Obanikoro
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The former Minister of State for Defence, Senator Musiliu Obanikoro, has declared that no political opponent can match the experience, competence and public service credentials of President Bola Tinubu and Lagos State Deputy Governor, Dr Kadri Obafemi Hamzat, ahead of the 2027 general elections. The

item.title: My covenant with the people of Lagos – Sanwo-Olu
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Reflecting on his journey to public office, Sanwo-Olu recalled that before becoming governor, he had spent decades working quietly in banking, the oil and gas sector, and public service, believing that true meaningful service is often rendered away from the spotlight, without fanfare.

item.title: FIFA ‘destroying football’ for the World Cup, says Spanish football chief
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: In a scathing interview, La Liga President Javier Tebas says FIFA chief Gianni Infantino’s time is 'over'.

item.description: The lawmaker representing Ilaje Constituency II in the Ondo State House of Assembly, Hon. Olawumi Fayemi-Obayelu, has alleged a threat to her life following an alleged plan to impeach the Speaker, Hon. Olamide Oladiji. Fayemi-Obayelu, in a statement, alleged that the Speaker of the Assembly was plan

item.title: Donald Kaberuka: From Rwandan refugee to architect of development finance
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: From Kigali to Tunis, this Rwandan has shaped the continent’s economy over three decades. A portrait of a pragmatist who has always favoured tailored solutions over a one-size-fits-all approach.

item.title: Trust you can feel: How OPay powers millions of financial decisions every day

item.title: The Light Flip is a minimalist flip phone with a point to prove

item.description: A few years ago, the team at Light shipped the most smartphone-like device they'd ever created. The Light Phone III still kept to its minimalist, no-distractions ethos, but it was a phone-size rectangle with a phone-size touchscreen, a camera, and a number of other features. Some people loved having

item.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Google is launching an AI security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI systems, such as the one offered by Anthro

item.title: Miatta Fahnbulleh: The Liberian refugee now leading Britain’s energy future
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Born in Monrovia and forced to flee Liberia’s civil war as a child, Miatta Fahnbulleh went from refugee to rising political star, earning the new Prime Minister Andy Burnham’s trust. Now, as energy secretary, she’s tasked with overhauling Britain’s energy system while easing pressure on household bi

item.title: SARMAAN: How Nigeria can turn child survival gains into stronger national policy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: More than 16 million children have now been reached through the SARMAAN Project across northern Nigeria, a milestone that says as much about system strength as it does about operational scale. The post SARMAAN: How Nigeria can turn child survival gains into stronger national policy appeared first on

item.title: Tax-friendly countries: Top destinations for high-net-worth Africans in 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Here are some of the world's most tax-friendly destinations in 2026. The post Tax-friendly countries: Top destinations for high-net-worth Africans in 2026 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Fake agency’s domiciliary accounts never active – CBN
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The CBN said the two foreign currency accounts opened for the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council were never activated because the agency failed to provide authorised signatories. The post Fake agency’s domiciliary accounts never active – CBN appeared first on Premium Times

item.title: How I was sexually molested at 12 — Peller
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: While speaking on the Honest Bunch podcast, the content creator recalled his childhood experiences, revealing how he lost his virginity. The post How I was sexually molested at 12 — Peller appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Police Arrest 1,198 Suspects, Rescue 166 Kidnap Victims In 1 Month
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force has announced the arrest of 1,198 criminal suspects nationwide in just over one month, alongside the rescue of 166 kidnapped victims. Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Olatunji Disu stated this on Tuesday at the monthly conference with strategic senior officers, noting that

item.title: Madagascar: One of the Most Important Places for Life on Earth - Madagascar
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica] Antananarivo -- Has a chance to lead the world in conservation, and species protection, and we must not waste It.

item.title: SpaceX’s Great Unlocking Begins With $116 Billion Share Release
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SpaceX has set the stage for one of the largest share unlocks in capital markets history, with as much as $116 billion worth of stock becoming eligible for sale for the first time next month.

item.title: Tell ex-wife to leave, I want to bring my sick mother home – Man begs court
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “Every day, there is trouble in the house. My oldest hits me now and doesn’t respect me as a father" The post Tell ex-wife to leave, I want to bring my sick mother home – Man begs court appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Max Air Paid ₦182m In Ticket Refunds To 956 Passengers In Q2 – NCAA
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Max Air refunded more than ₦182 million to 956 passengers in the second quarter of 2026, according to the Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA). The post Max Air Paid ₦182m In Ticket Refunds To 956 Passengers In Q2 – NCAA appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: After TikTok, Snap settles social media addiction case
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: TikTok recently settled its portion of the case with the plaintiff ahead of a jury trial that starts next week in Los Angeles. YouTube has also reached a deal, leaving Meta as the only remaining defendant.

item.title: FIFA destroying football industry – La Liga chief
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The president of the Spanish football league, Javier Tebas, accused FIFA of acting "irresponsibly" following the announcement of discussions regarding a possible 64-team World Cup in 2030, in an interview with an Italian sports daily published Tuesday. The post FIFA destroying football industry &#82

item.title: Why Paramount could win if it loses the Warner Bros. Discovery deal
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: States are trying to block Paramount’s mega-merger. Shareholders should root for them.

item.title: Atiku to Tinubu: Don’t drag God into your budget racket
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Former Vice President of Nigeria and Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, has said the Tinubu administration must resist the dangerous temptation to drag priests, pastors, imams and other religious leaders into its budgeting racket. The post Atiku to T
